target: data/sections.json part2b — SEC-2b.1 stratification paragraph and TLDR
summary: Fixes four factual errors in the SEC-2b.1 stratification paragraph and TLDR, verified against current wins.json. The curmudgeon c2 review identified miscounted self-contradicted WINs (9 claimed, 8 actual with monitoring=none), incorrect WIN list (included 3 hardcoded WINs while missing 2 none WINs), wrong misleading count ({{TALLY_MISLEADING}} renders as 23 but context requires 15 misleading+none WINs), and TLDR overgeneralizing the 50% error floor to all 4 live-fetch WINs when only the NMP Drift Rate domain has it. Also strengthens the contrast sentence: instead of claiming the dome ignores self-contradicted WINs, reveals that 8 self-contradicted WINs are actively hardcoded to pass — a stronger argument (designed masking vs. negligent omission).

patches.description: Fix TLDR 50% error floor overgeneralization and terminology (monitored domains → WINs)
patches.old_text: Only {{CA_LIVE}} WINs fetch live external data, and even those use adaptive tolerances with 50% error floors.
patches.new_text: Only {{CA_LIVE}} WINs fetch live external data — all from globe-calibrated sources — and the NMP Drift Rate domain uses an adaptive tolerance with a 50% error floor.
patches.rationale: The 50% error floor (effective_tol = max(adaptive_tol, 50.0)) is documented only for the NMP Drift Rate domain (WIN-006/007). WIN-004 (SAA separation) and WIN-024 (Roaring 40s) have different tolerance settings. Claiming all 4 have 50% floors is factually checkable and wrong — a dome defender who inspects WIN-004 tolerance settings would find no 50% floor, undermining TLDR credibility. The replacement is precise about which domain has the floor while adding the globe-calibrated-sources point (which applies to all 4 and is the section body's main argument about live-fetch WINs).

patches.description: Fix self-contradicted count (9→8), WIN list (remove hardcoded, add missing), misleading count, and add fuller breakdown
patches.old_text: Stratifying the unmonitored WINs: The {{CA_NONE}} WINs with no monitoring code are not all equivalent failures. Two are theological assertions with no measurable content (WIN-032 New Jerusalem, WIN-034 copper firmament) — monitoring is not applicable. Nine are self-contradicted by the dome's own geometry (WIN-045/046/049/050/051 tidal, WIN-053 flux, WIN-056 solar, WIN-061 P-wave, WIN-067 gravity) — monitoring would expose the contradiction. {{TALLY_MISLEADING}} are misleading (duplicated, circular, or non-discriminating). The remaining WINs are quantitative predictions the dome could monitor but chooses not to. The absence is most telling for the self-contradicted WINs: the dome has built monitoring infrastructure for {{CA_HARDCODED}} WINs (all hardcoded to pass) but does not monitor the 9 WINs whose own geometry produces wrong answers.
patches.new_text:
Stratifying the unmonitored WINs: The {{CA_NONE}} WINs with no monitoring code are not all equivalent failures. Two are theological assertions with no measurable content (WIN-032, WIN-034) — monitoring is not applicable. Eight are self-contradicted by the dome's own geometry (WIN-044/050/051 tidal, WIN-053 flux, WIN-056 solar, WIN-061 P-wave, WIN-067 gravity, WIN-068 tidal model) — monitoring would expose the contradiction. Fifteen are misleading (duplicated, circular, or non-discriminating). Eleven are standard-physics relabellings — monitoring would confirm the phenomenon but not the dome's causal explanation. Three are refuted by published data — monitoring would expose the failure. Two are not demonstrated. The gaps are systematic: the dome's monitoring avoids exactly the categories where testing would be most damaging.

The contrast with the hardcoded WINs is even more revealing. Of the dome's 16 self-contradicted WINs (Part 2), eight have no monitoring at all — but the other eight (WIN-002/010/012/029/038/045/046/049) do have monitoring domains, all hardcoded to pass. The dome does not merely neglect to test its geometric contradictions; for half of them, it builds validation infrastructure that cannot fail by construction. Whether omitted or hardcoded, no self-contradicted WIN faces a genuine empirical test.
patches.rationale: Four factual corrections: (1) Count changed from 9 to 8 because WIN-045, WIN-046, WIN-049 have monitoring=hardcoded, not monitoring=none. WIN-044 and WIN-068 were missing from the list (both monitoring=none, verdict=Self-Contradicted). (2) {{TALLY_MISLEADING}} template variable = 23 (all Misleading WINs) but this paragraph discusses only unmonitored WINs — the correct count is 15 (Misleading + monitoring=none). Replaced with hardcoded "Fifteen" since no template variable exists for this cross-tab; recommend decider create {{CA_MISLEADING_NONE}} template var. (3) Added the full verdict breakdown of unmonitored WINs (11 Std Model Explains, 3 Refuted by Data, 2 Not Demonstrated) per curmudgeon recommendation — sharpens the "systematic gaps" argument. (4) Split the contrast into its own paragraph and flipped the framing: instead of claiming the dome ignores self-contradicted WINs (factually wrong — 8 are hardcoded), the new text reveals that the dome actively monitors them with unfalsifiable checks. This is a stronger argument: designed masking vs negligent omission.
patches.build_note: IMPORTANT: This patch hardcodes "Eight", "Fifteen", "Eleven", "Three", "Two", and "16" because no template variables exist for verdict+monitoring cross-tabs. Recommend decider create these template variables in generate-html.js to avoid staleness on future WIN additions. Minimum needed: {{CA_SC_NONE}} (self-contradicted+none), {{CA_MISLEADING_NONE}} (misleading+none), {{CA_SME_NONE}} (std-model+none), {{CA_RBD_NONE}} (refuted+none), {{CA_ND_NONE}} (not-demonstrated+none), {{CA_SC_HARDCODED}} (self-contradicted+hardcoded). Until then, these counts must be verified on any WIN addition.

holes_addressed: TLDR overgeneralizes 50% error floor to all 4 live-fetch WINs (curmudgeon major hole #1)
Self-contradicted WINs miscounted: 9→8, wrong WIN IDs listed (curmudgeon major hole #2)
Misleading WIN count wrong: {{TALLY_MISLEADING}}=23 but context needs 15 (curmudgeon major hole #3)
Contrast sentence factually wrong: 8 self-contradicted WINs ARE hardcoded-monitored (curmudgeon major hole #4)
Missing verdict categories in stratification: Std Model Explains (11), Refuted by Data (3), Not Demonstrated (2) (curmudgeon moderate hole #6)
TLDR terminology: "monitored domains" → corrected in TLDR rewrite (curmudgeon minor hole #7)
new_evidence_added: Full verdict×monitoring cross-tab from wins.json (8 SC+none, 8 SC+hardcoded, 15 M+none, 11 SME+none, 3 RBD+none, 2 ND+none, 2 UF+none)
Specific WIN IDs for the 8 hardcoded self-contradicted WINs (WIN-002/010/012/029/038/045/046/049)

anticipated_objections.objection: Dome defender: The hardcoded values ARE the predictions — comparing them to known values IS validation.
anticipated_objections.response: Validation means testing against independently obtained data. Comparing a static number to itself tests nothing — it confirms the prediction was entered correctly into the spreadsheet, not that nature agrees. If a prediction and its observation are both typed into the same source file, the check is tautological.

anticipated_objections.objection: Dome defender: Some hardcoded WINs have complex derivations behind them — the monitoring confirms the derivation was done correctly.
anticipated_objections.response: The monitoring confirms only that the final number matches, not the derivation. A wrong derivation that reaches the right number by coincidence (or by fitting) would pass identically. Real validation would re-derive from current input data each run.

anticipated_objections.objection: Dome defender: The stratification breakdown is cherry-picked — the unmonitored WINs are just the ones that don't need monitoring.
anticipated_objections.response: Self-contradicted WINs are precisely the ones that most need monitoring — they're the claims where the dome's own equations produce wrong answers. If monitoring confirmed these predictions, it would prove the geometry works despite the apparent contradictions. The dome chose not to test them, and for the ones it did build checks for, it hardcoded them to pass.

kernel_of_truth: The dome's monitoring infrastructure is technically functional — the 4 live-fetch domains do pull real data from NOAA and INTERMAGNET, the pipeline runs every 5 minutes, and the status_history.json records pass/fail states over time. This is more monitoring infrastructure than most alternative cosmological models build. The problem is not that the dome lacks a monitoring system — it's that the system is designed in a way that makes failure structurally impossible for the vast majority of WINs.
deferred_issues: The accuracy arithmetic (69/(69+4+8) ≈ 85.2%) may need template variables — curmudgeon flagged as potentially stale (moderate hole #5). Deferred to separate expansion since it requires build script changes.
OPEN-001 through OPEN-015 count ("15 unresolved open problems") is unchecked against current dome site — may have changed. Curmudgeon flagged as unverified citation.
The 39 monitoring domains count should be verified against current V51.1 monitor.py — may have changed since V51.0 commit 8e4bf9b.
